metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, Dized interactive rules, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 shared **Basecamp board** (center of table) — holds card display and glory track
- **20 hexagonal location tiles** in 3 regions:
  - 6 South (face-up at game start)
  - 7 Central (face-down)
  - 7 North (face-down)
- 1 **mech mat** per player — tracks power, guile, workers, and action token position

#### Cards
- **123 uniquely illustrated cards** in a single shuffled deck, 3 types:
  - **Item cards** — instant benefit when played, plus ongoing ability; can be tucked under your mat as permanent upgrades
  - **Meteorite cards** — meld ability that grows more powerful with each tucked meteorite (cascading bonuses)
  - **Quest cards** — require your mech at a specific location to solve; tuck under your mat for major scoring
- Each player also starts with a **character card** and a **companion card** in hand (your opening engine)

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- 1 large **mech miniature** per player (snaps onto silicone base)
- 4 **glory tokens** (stars) per player — placed via Boast to trigger endgame
- 1 **action token** (small cube) per player — sits on mech mat, controls available actions
- Power and guile tracking tokens per player
- 50 **worker meeples** in 5 colors (shared supply)
- 36 **corruption tiles** in a cloth bag (worth money at game end)
- Map tokens and coins (limited supply)

#### Other Components
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)
- 2 player aid cards per player
- Custom insert with setup tray

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Overview
- Standalone sequel to Scythe, set in an alternate 1920s
- A meteorite has crashed near the Tunguska River, scattering corruption across the wilderness
- You pilot a mech to explore locations, play cards to build your engine, gather resources, and vanquish corruption for money

#### Your 4 Actions
- Mech mat has 4 action spaces: **Move**, **Play**, **Gather**, and **Refresh**
- Your action token (small cube) covers one space — that action is unavailable this turn

**After a Refresh:**
- Action token sits on Refresh space; shift it left into the Move/Play/Gather area
- All 3 actions are uncovered — perform all 3 in any order (your big turn)

**Subsequent turns:**
- Move the action token to cover exactly 1 of the 3 action spaces
- Perform the other 2 in any order
- Must cover a *different* space than last turn — no repeating the same skip

**Refresh turn:**
- Return all active row cards to hand and all workers to mat
- Place action token on Refresh space
- **Key insight** — Refreshing costs a full turn of production; stretch cycles as long as possible

#### The Card Engine
- **Hand** (left of mech mat) — cards waiting to be played
- **Active row** (right of mech mat) — cards in play, generating abilities when you place workers on them
- **Tucked** (under mech mat edges) — upgraded items, melded meteorites, solved quests; score permanently and never leave

#### Corruption: A Resource, Not a Punishment
- Corruption is something you **want** — each token is worth **$2 at game end**
- When you explore a face-down tile, corruption tokens get placed on it from the bag
- **Vanquish** corruption by spending power and guile, then **collect** the token
- Having **7+ corruption tokens** qualifies for a glory category

#### Game End
- When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, endgame triggers
- Every player (including the trigger player) takes exactly 1 final turn
- Most money wins

#### Checkpoint
- Mech adjacent to Basecamp
- Character + companion cards in hand
- Action token on Refresh
- Power and guile both at zero

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. **Place Basecamp board** centrally; arrange 6 South location tiles face-up per layout diagram
2. **Shuffle Central (7) and North (7) tiles** face-down in their spots; place 1 map token on each face-down tile
3. **Shuffle all item, meteorite, and quest cards** into one deck; deal 5 face-up onto Basecamp display
4. **Fill corruption bag** with all tokens except the 20-value token (set aside for Location 20)
5. **Each player takes**: mech mat + mech miniature, character + companion card (into hand), 4 glory tokens, action token (on Refresh), power/guile at zero
6. **Place all mechs** adjacent to Basecamp; randomly select first player

#### MOVE — Exploring the Map
- Move your mech **1–3 spaces** (Marsh Strider gets 1–4)
- Can pass through occupied spaces, but **cannot end on one**
- Once you leave Basecamp, you can never return
- **Revealing tiles** — when you move onto a face-down tile:
  1. Movement ends immediately
  2. Pick up the map token
  3. Flip tile face-up
  4. Draw corruption tokens from the bag until their total value meets or exceeds the location's printed value — stack them on the location

#### PLAY — Building Your Engine
- Play exactly **1 card** from hand to the rightmost position in your active row
- Then, in any order:
  - **Gain the core value** (upper-left number) — advance power or guile track (max 10)
  - **Activate the card's ability** — place a matching-color worker from your mat onto the card
- **Rules**: worker color must match the card's requirement; each card's ability activates at most once per turn

#### GATHER — Collecting Location Benefits
- Gain **all exposed benefits** at your mech's current location
- Corruption tokens cover benefits underneath until vanquished
- You **must** gain all exposed benefits if possible — no cherry-picking
- Benefits include: gaining a card from display, sweeping display, gaining a worker, gaining a map token, playing a card, upgrading an item, melding a meteorite, boasting, and others

#### REFRESH — Resetting Your Engine
- Return all active row cards to hand and all workers to mat
- Place action token on Refresh space
- **Costs an entire turn** — build your active row to 4–5 cards before resetting whenever possible

#### Vanquish — Clearing Corruption for Profit
- Triggered through card abilities and location benefits (not a standalone action)
- **Pay the cost** on the topmost corruption token at your location using power and/or guile
- **Take the token** — worth **$2 at game end**
- May repeat to vanquish additional tokens at the same location
- Removing the final corruption from a location unlocks the hidden benefit for Gathering
- **Location 20** — requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or); still only $2, but earns a glory category
- Having **7+ corruption tokens** qualifies for a glory category

#### Solve — Completing Quests
- Your mech must be at the location matching the quest card
- Pay the power/guile cost shown; gain the benefit
- Tuck the quest under the top edge of your mech mat (max 4 solved)
- **Solving 3+ quests** raises every glory star from $5 to **$10** — a $20 swing across 4 stars

#### Upgrade — Locking In Item Abilities
- Tuck an item from hand or active row under the right edge of your mech mat
- Ongoing ability becomes permanently active (no worker needed)
- Max 4 upgraded items; coin value on the item scores at game end

#### Meld — Cascading Meteorite Bonuses
- Tuck a meteorite under the bottom edge of your mech mat
- **Cascading** — you gain the meld bonus of ALL currently melded meteorites, not just the new one:
  - 1st meld = 1 bonus; 2nd = 2; 3rd = 3; 4th = all 4 at once
- Max 4 melded meteorites (Tatanka gets 5)

#### Boast — Placing Your Glory Stars
- When you've achieved a glory category, place a glory star on it
- **7 categories**: Quests (4 solved), Meteorites (4 melded), Items (4 upgraded), Location 20 (vanquished), Corruption (7+ tokens), Cards (8+ controlled), Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ map tokens)
- Multiple players can have stars on the same category
- You need only **4 of 7** categories to trigger endgame

#### Mech Abilities
- **Tatanka** — 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades (instead of 4); go wider than anyone else
- **Marsh Strider** — movement range 1–4 (instead of 1–3); reach distant locations first
- **Lumberjack** — reduce first corruption cost per color by 1; cheaper vanquishing
- **Highlander** — gain cards to hand instead of active row; more flexibility on timing
- **Odin's Wrath** — trash a map token for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1; exploration fuels your engine

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
- When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, endgame begins
- Every player (including the trigger player) takes exactly **1 final turn**
- A well-planned final turn can swing $10–15

#### Final Scoring
- Everything converts to money; richest explorer wins
- **Coins** — face value
- **Upgraded items** — coin value printed on card
- **Corruption tokens** — $2 each (including the 20-value token)
- **Glory stars** — variable, scales with quests solved:
  - 0 quests = $5 per star
  - 1 quest = $6 per star
  - 2 quests = $8 per star
  - 3+ quests = **$10 per star**
- With 4 stars and 3 solved quests, stars alone = $40; with 0 quests, only $20

#### Tiebreaker
1. Sum of all glory category values (totals across every category, including those without stars)
2. If still tied: shared victory

#### Typical Score Ranges
- First game: $35–50
- Experienced play: $55–75
- Highly optimized: $80+

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Minimize Your Refresh Turns
- Every Refresh is a full turn of zero production while everyone else advances
- **Ideal: 4–5 turns of productivity** between each Refresh
- Build your active row to 4–5 cards before resetting
- Refreshing every 2 turns = losing the game in tempo

#### Solve 3 Quests — Your Top Priority
- Solving 3+ quests raises each glory star from $5 to **$10**
- Across 4 stars, that's a **$20 swing** — the difference between winning and losing
- Even 2 quests ($8/star) is a major improvement

#### Plan Your Quest Setup Multiple Turns Ahead
- Solving a quest requires 4 conditions simultaneously:
  1. You have a quest card
  2. Your mech is at the matching location
  3. You have enough power/guile to pay the cost
  4. You play a card with a Solve ability that turn
- Start positioning early — this alignment takes multi-turn planning

#### Meld Meteorites for Cascading Value
- Each meld triggers bonuses from ALL melded meteorites, not just the new one
- 4th meld triggers all 4 bonuses at once = 10 total bonus triggers across 4 melds
- Front-load meteorite collection so cascading compounds early

#### Vanquish Corruption Aggressively
- **$2 each** at game end; **7+ tokens** qualifies for a glory category
- That's potentially $14+ in tokens plus a star worth $5–10
- Invest in power and guile early for efficient vanquishing

#### Leverage Your Mech's Unique Ability
- **Tatanka** — 5th slot gives more flexibility than anyone else
- **Marsh Strider** — extra movement range (1–4) reaches distant locations first
- **Highlander** — cards to hand instead of active row gives timing control
- **Lumberjack** — reduced corruption costs = most efficient vanquisher; lean into corruption glory
- **Odin's Wrath** — convert excess map tokens into power, guile, or money

#### Choose Your 4 Glory Paths Based on the Board
- You need only 4 of 7 categories
- Read board state, card draws, and opponents' progress
- Pick the 4 that align with your engine — don't chase all 7

#### Watch for the Endgame Trigger
- Game ends when anyone places their 4th glory star
- If an opponent is at 3 stars, accelerate your Boasts or lock in your final turn scoring
- Getting caught off-guard by a sudden endgame is one of the most common ways to lose

#### Common Mistakes
- **Refreshing too often** — every Refresh is a lost turn; build larger active rows
- **Ignoring quests** — 0 quests = $20 from stars instead of $40; biggest scoring mistake possible
- **Avoiding corruption** — $2 each and counts toward a glory category; don't leave free money on the board
- **Spreading across all 7 categories** — focus on 4 achievable categories rather than half-completing 6
- **Forgetting worker color matching** — workers must match the card's color requirement; plan allocation before playing
- **Skipping location benefits** — you MUST gain all exposed benefits when Gathering; plan movement to land on helpful locations

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Expeditions

**Before You Start**: Mech adjacent to Basecamp, character + companion cards in hand, action token on Refresh, power and guile at zero. 6 South locations face-up; Central and North tiles face-down with map tokens.

#### Turn 1 — The Big Opening (All 3 Actions)
1. Shift action token left into the Move/Play/Gather area — all 3 actions available
2. **Move** your mech to a nearby South location
3. **Play** your character card to active row — gain its core value (advance power or guile), activate its ability by placing a matching-color worker
4. **Gather** at your location — pick up an exposed benefit (card from display, new worker, etc.)

**Checkpoint**: 1 card in active row, 1 worker deployed, some power or guile on your tracker

#### Turn 2 — Choosing What to Skip (2 Actions)
- Shift action token to cover one of the three spaces:
  - **Cover Move** — stay put, Play + Gather
  - **Cover Gather** — Move + Play somewhere new
  - **Cover Play** — Move + Gather without adding to your engine
- Choose based on what your location offers and what's in your hand

#### Turns 3–5 — Explore and Build
- Play item and meteorite cards to grow your active row
- Move onto face-down tiles to explore — flip them, gain map tokens, watch corruption stack on revealed locations
- Start building power and guile for vanquishing corruption ($2 per token)

#### Mid-Game — Your Engine Running
- Active row should have 3–4 cards generating abilities each turn
- When workers are all placed and hand is low, take a Refresh turn
- Next turn after Refresh = all-3-actions turn

#### Quest Solving
- Position mech at the matching location with enough power/guile to pay the cost
- Play a card with a Solve ability — quest tucks under your mat permanently
- **Aim for 3+ solved quests** — glory star value jumps from $5 to $10 each

#### Vanquishing Corruption
- At a location with corruption tokens, use card abilities to vanquish
- Pay the power/guile cost on the topmost token, then take it ($2 at game end)
- Clear all corruption to unlock the hidden benefit for future Gather actions

#### Endgame Push
- Once you've achieved 4 glory categories (e.g., 4 solved quests, 4 upgraded items, 7+ corruption tokens, 8+ cards controlled), find Boast benefits via Gather and place your 4 stars
- Game ends after everyone takes 1 final turn

#### Scoring
- **Add**: coins + upgraded item values + $2 per corruption token + glory star values (scaled by quests solved)
- First-game target: **$40–55** is solid

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Action Token Optimization
- You get all 3 actions after Refresh, then 2 per turn until next Refresh
- **Ideal cycle: 4–5 turns per Refresh** — shorter wastes Refresh turns; longer leaves hand empty and workers stuck
- **Cover Move** — when your location has multiple benefits; stay put to Play + Gather
- **Cover Gather** — when you need to reposition; Move + Play lets you travel and build engine
- **Cover Play** — when a location has Boast or Vanquish benefits; Move + Gather to reach and collect

#### Quest-First Engine Building
- Solving 3+ quests = every glory star worth **$10 instead of $5** ($20 swing across 4 stars)
- Structure your entire early game around quest completion:
  1. **Acquire quest cards early** from Basecamp display or via Gather benefits
  2. **Plot movement toward quest locations** while exploring nearby tiles
  3. **Build power and guile** through character, companion, and item cards
  4. **Time your Play action** so a Solve ability is ready when your mech reaches the quest location
- Payoff: 3 solved quests + 4 stars = $40 in stars alone

#### Corruption as a Scoring Engine
- **$2 each** + **7+ tokens** earns a glory star; 8 tokens = $16 + a star worth $5–10
- **Lumberjack** — natural advantage with reduced corruption costs
- **Invest in both power AND guile** — most tokens accept either, but Location 20 requires both at max
- **Explore aggressively** to spawn corruption, then vanquish before opponents arrive

#### Meld Cascading
- 1st meld = 1 bonus; 2nd = 2; 3rd = 3; 4th = all 4 at once (10 total triggers across 4 melds)
- **Front-load meteorite collection** so cascading compounds early
- **Pair complementary bonuses** — power meld + guile meld accelerates vanquishing
- **Tatanka's 5th slot** — 5 cascading melds = 15 total bonus triggers

#### Glory Path Selection
- You need 4 of 7 categories:
  - **Quests (4 solved)** — always pursue; scoring multiplier affects ALL stars; most impactful category
  - **Corruption (7+ tokens)** — strong with Lumberjack or aggressive exploration
  - **Items (4 upgraded)** — natural byproduct of engine building; easy if drawing lots of items
  - **Meteorites (4 melded)** — cascading bonuses make this self-reinforcing; especially strong with Tatanka
  - **Cards (8+ controlled)** — count hand + active row + tucked cards; often reachable passively
  - **Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ maps)** — maps from exploring face-down tiles; workers from Gather benefits
  - **Location 20** — requires maxing both power and guile; high cost but only 1 vanquish to qualify

#### Endgame Trigger Timing
- Game ends when someone places their 4th star; everyone gets exactly 1 final turn
- **Trigger early when ahead in money** — ending the game locks in your advantage
- **Delay by not Boasting** — if you need 1–2 more turns for quests or high-value corruption
- **Watch opponents at 3 stars** — if someone is one Boast away, make sure your final turn is already planned

rulings: The action token stays in the Move/Play/Gather box once placed there. You must Refresh to return to all-3-actions mode.
'Gain 1 location benefit' means exactly 1 benefit icon, even if the location shows multiple icons.
A card's ability may only activate once per turn unless the card specifically states otherwise.
Adjacency is always relative to the mech's physical position on the map, not to cards in your active row.
'Gain the core value of all solved quests' includes the quest being solved in the same action.
You must gain ALL exposed benefits when Gathering if possible. You cannot selectively skip them.
Power and guile cap at 10. Effects that would push above 10 stop at the cap.
The 20-value corruption requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). It is still only worth $2 at game end.
Worker color must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability.
Gaining a Refresh benefit from a card is NOT the same as taking the Refresh action — your action token does NOT move to the Refresh space.
